meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Palestinian leader Barghouti endures ‘violent’ assaults in Israeli prisons
items.summary: Recent reports of prison brutality follow an attack last year that resulted in broken ribs and head injuries.

items.title: ‘Food is unifying’: How Lebanon’s migrants are feeding the war-displaced
items.summary: Often overlooked in Lebanon's society, the migrant community is filling the gaps of the state by feeding their own.

items.title: Families across Sudan endure years of displacement, hunger, loss
items.summary: More than nine million people remain displaced in Sudan, while nearly 29 million face acute hunger.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 47 of the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has signalled a possible second round of talks with Iran in the coming days.

items.title: Trump turns on Meloni, saying she lacks ‘courage’ over US-Israel war on Iran
items.summary: The US president says he is 'shocked at her', delivering a blunt public rebuke to one of his closest European allies.

items.title: Uzbek chess grandmaster Sindarov sets up world title match with Gukesh
items.summary: Javokhir Sindarov wins the Candidates Tournament with ⁠a round ⁠to spare and will face India's Gukesh next.

items.title: China’s Xi meets Russian FM Lavrov, calls relations with Moscow ‘precious’
items.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov met with top Chinese leadership to discuss the Iran war and other concerns.

items.title: Iran says $270bn war loss must be compensated, as fresh talks with US loom
items.summary: Iran demands compensation for war damages caused by US and Israeli attacks on critical infrastructure.

items.title: England beat Spain 1-0 to lead Women’s World Cup 2027 qualifying group
items.summary: Hemp scores to give the Euro champions a crucial win as Keira Walsh leads in the absence of recovering Leah Williamson.

items.title: Avdija scores 41 as Trail Blazers upset Suns in NBA West play-in
items.summary: Deni Avdija sends Portland into the NBA playoffs after a shock road win against Phoenix in the 7th vs 8th play-in tie.

items.title: Candidate calls for new election in Peru, claiming fraud without evidence
items.summary: Peru’s presidential candidate Rafael Lopez Aliaga is urging officials to declare Sunday’s election 'null and void’.

items.title: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
items.summary: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

items.title: South Africa appoints former apartheid-era negotiator as US ambassador
items.summary: Roelf Meyer will replace the South African ambassador who was expelled from the US by President Donald Trump in 2025.

items.title: New trial begins over football legend Maradona’s death
items.summary: A new trial has begun in Argentina over the death of football legend Diego Maradona.

items.title: US forces kill 4 people in latest strike on vessels in eastern Pacific
items.summary: The killings mark the fourth US deadly strike in the past four days on vessels in the eastern Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon hold direct talks for first time in decades
items.summary: After the first direct talks in decades, Israel and Lebanon have agreed to begin negotiations.

items.title: Israeli attacks kill 11, including two children, in day of strikes on Gaza
items.summary: A three-year-old and a 14-year-old were among those killed in Israel's latest strikes on northern Gaza.

items.title: Haiti’s Culture Ministry fires workers over citadel stampede that killed 25
items.summary: At least nine people have been arrested following the stampede, including police officers and ministry employees.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump says war ‘close to over’ as Hormuz blockade continues
items.summary: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as naval blockade of Iranian ports continues.

items.title: French police arrest students protesting anti-Semitism law
items.summary: Police in France have arrested students in Paris during a sit-in against a controversial anti-Semitism bill.

items.title: Delcy Rodriguez calls for a ‘Venezuela free of sanctions’ amid US detente
items.summary: Venezuelan president says limited sanctions relief has not been enough to stabilise the country's turbulent economy.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
items.summary: The meeting marks the first direct talks between the two sides, though US officials say more time is needed.

items.title: The Take: Who is keeping the fight alive in Sudan’s war?
items.summary: What three years of war have done to Sudan.

items.title: Fifth woman accuses former US lawmaker Eric Swalwell of sexual misconduct
items.summary: The Democratic representative has resigned his seat in Congress over multiple sexual misconduct allegations.

items.title: Atletico edge Barcelona on aggregate to reach Champions League semifinal
items.summary: Atletico Madrid advance 3-2 on aggregate, despite a 2-1 home defeat by Barcelona, in a thrilling encounter.
